description: An authorized sequel to Wyndham's Day of the Triffids, set decades after the original disaster.

title: The Land of Laughs
author: Jonathan Carroll
description: A schoolteacher visits the small Missouri town of a beloved dead children's author and discovers the entire town is his invention.

title: Bones of the Moon
author: Jonathan Carroll
description: A woman's vivid dream-world of Rondua begins to infiltrate — and threaten — her waking New York life.

title: Outside the Dog Museum
author: Jonathan Carroll
description: A celebrated architect is hired to build a temple to dogs for an Arabian sultan and confronts the edge of his sanity.

title: White Apples
author: Jonathan Carroll
description: A recently deceased womanizer is returned to life for reasons the universe itself urgently needs him to understand.

title: On Immunity
author: Eula Biss
description: An inoculation of essays threading vaccines, motherhood, and the myth of the individual body.

title: Having and Being Had
author: Eula Biss
description: Essays on capitalism, homeownership, and the guilt of entering the middle class.

title: Notes from No Man's Land
author: Eula Biss
description: Essays on American race, moving to Chicago, and the thin histories we carry.

title: How to Do Nothing
author: Jenny Odell
description: A manifesto against the attention economy that insists paying attention to the natural world is resistance.

title: Saving Time
author: Jenny Odell
description: An argument that the clock-based view of time is neither natural nor inevitable.

title: The Lonely City
author: Olivia Laing
description: Braided essays on loneliness and urban art — Hopper, Warhol, Henry Darger — written from Laing's own lonely New York.

title: The Trip to Echo Spring
author: Olivia Laing
description: A journey across America following six great drunk American writers and their shared disease.

title: Crudo
author: Olivia Laing
description: A woman named Kathy Acker gets married in summer 2017 while the world appears to end around her.

title: Everybody
author: Olivia Laing
description: A book-length essay on the body, freedom, and the thinkers who tried to make sense of both.

title: A Knight in Shining Armor
author: Jude Deveraux
description: A modern American woman abandoned by her fiancé in England weeps on a sixteenth-century knight's tomb — and calls him back to life.

title: The Velvet Promise
author: Jude Deveraux
description: The first Montgomery family novel: a medieval lord's arranged marriage turns into a long fight for trust.

title: The Maiden
author: Jude Deveraux
description: A fierce warrior queen from a tiny Scandinavian country marries into the Montgomery family.

title: The Bride
author: Julie Garwood
description: An English noblewoman is married off to a Scottish laird by order of the king and slowly wins her way into his clan.

title: The Secret
author: Julie Garwood
description: Childhood friends from opposite sides of the Scottish border are reunited as adults when one of them needs to save her sister.

title: Ransom
author: Julie Garwood
description: A Scottish highlander and an English lady get caught up in a kidnapping during the time of King John.

title: Devil's Bride
author: Stephanie Laurens
description: The first Cynster novel: a rakish Devil Cynster is forced into a marriage of convenience that neither of them expected to survive.

title: A Secret Love
author: Stephanie Laurens
description: A young widowed baroness hides her new love affair from her meddling Cynster cousins.

title: On a Wild Night
author: Stephanie Laurens
description: An independent heiress and a rakish Cynster collide while searching for the same missing woman.

title: Rafe
author: Rebekah Weatherspoon
description: A billionaire single mom hires a rugged cowboy nanny for her twins in the first Cowboys of California novel.

title: Xeni
author: Rebekah Weatherspoon
description: A woman inherits her aunt's Vermont estate on the condition that she marry the Scottish handyman within a month.

title: The Umbrella Academy: Apocalypse Suite
author: Gerard Way
description: Seven estranged siblings raised as superheroes reunite for their father's funeral as the world begins to end.

title: The Umbrella Academy: Dallas
author: Gerard Way
description: The Umbrella siblings are drawn into the assassination of JFK — which one of them may have to commit.

title: The Umbrella Academy: Hotel Oblivion
author: Gerard Way
description: The villains the Monocle imprisoned in a hotel between dimensions finally break out.

title: Arzach
author: Moebius
description: Wordless adventures of a silent warrior riding a pterodactyl through a strange alien landscape.

title: The Incal
author: Moebius
description: A cosmic science-fantasy epic written by Jodorowsky and drawn by Moebius — the class-three detective John Difool and the black Incal.

title: The Airtight Garage
author: Moebius
description: A surreal, stream-of-consciousness science-fiction story set inside a vast artificial world built by a renegade scientist.

title: My Favorite Thing Is Monsters Book One
author: Emil Ferris
description: A ten-year-old girl in 1968 Chicago believes she's a werewolf and investigates the death of her Holocaust-survivor neighbor in a notebook drawn with ballpoint pen.

title: My Favorite Thing Is Monsters Book Two
author: Emil Ferris
description: The long-awaited conclusion to Ferris's ballpoint-pen masterpiece brings Karen's investigation to its painful revelations.

title: On a Sunbeam
author: Tillie Walden
description: A restoration crew travels through a strange interstellar future, mending ruined buildings and, eventually, lost loves.

title: Are You Listening?
author: Tillie Walden
description: Two women drive across a dreamlike Texas carrying a cat that is not quite what it seems.

title: Clementine Book One
author: Tillie Walden
description: A young amputee survivor of a zombie apocalypse sets out with a new companion across a ruined world.

title: Sabrina
author: Nick Drnaso
description: A young woman disappears and the video footage that may explain her death unfolds into a study of loneliness and conspiracy culture.

title: Acting Class
author: Nick Drnaso
description: Ten strangers sign up for an acting class led by an unsettling instructor whose exercises blur the line between performance and reality.

title: Tamara Drewe
author: Posy Simmonds
description: A riff on Hardy's Far from the Madding Crowd set in a modern Dorset writer's retreat, in elegant long-form cartoons.

title: Gemma Bovery
author: Posy Simmonds
description: A rural Norman baker watches an English couple named Bovery move in next door and worries about literary fate.

title: Cassandra Darke
author: Posy Simmonds
description: A disgraced London art dealer blunders into a case of murder and trafficking in the winter snow.

title: Fortunata and Jacinta
author: Benito Pérez Galdós
description: Two women — a working-class beauty and a bourgeois wife — share a husband in nineteenth-century Madrid, the masterwork of Spanish realism.

title: Tristana
author: Benito Pérez Galdós
description: A young orphan becomes the ward — and then lover — of an aging rake who refuses to let her go.

title: Nazarín
author: Benito Pérez Galdós
description: A Christlike priest wanders the roads of Spain with two repentant prostitutes and is taken for a madman.

title: La Regenta
author: Leopoldo Alas
description: A young wife in a provincial Spanish city drifts toward adultery while her confessor and a cynical dandy both pursue her.

title: The Tree of Knowledge
author: Pío Baroja
description: A young Spanish medical student's idealism is ground down by the brutalities of turn-of-the-century Madrid and provincial medicine.

title: The Restlessness of Shanti Andía
author: Pío Baroja
description: An aging Basque sea captain recalls a life of voyages, smugglers, and lost loves.

title: Mist
author: Miguel de Unamuno
description: A hopelessly lovesick man visits the author of his own existence to argue about whether he is allowed to die.

title: Abel Sánchez
author: Miguel de Unamuno
description: A modern retelling of Cain and Abel through two boyhood friends consumed by jealousy.

title: Saint Manuel Bueno, Martyr
author: Miguel de Unamuno
description: A village priest secretly loses his faith but continues to serve his people with greater care than ever.

title: Marks of Identity
author: Juan Goytisolo
description: A Spanish exile in Barcelona and Paris shuffles documents of his family past in an attempt to make sense of Franco's Spain.

title: Count Julian
author: Juan Goytisolo
description: An exiled Spaniard in Tangier imagines a ferocious Moorish reconquest of Spain.

title: Juan the Landless
author: Juan Goytisolo
description: The final volume of the Álvaro Mendiola trilogy breaks open into pure textual iconoclasm.

title: Soldiers of Salamis
author: Javier Cercas
description: A journalist investigates the story of a Falangist writer who was spared by an anonymous Republican soldier in the final days of the Spanish Civil War.

title: The Anatomy of a Moment
author: Javier Cercas
description: A minute-by-minute reconstruction of the 1981 attempted coup that nearly toppled Spanish democracy.

title: The Impostor
author: Javier Cercas
description: Cercas investigates the most famous liar in modern Spain — a man who falsely claimed to be a Nazi concentration camp survivor.

title: Sepharad
author: Antonio Muñoz Molina
description: Seventeen interwoven stories of exile across twentieth-century Europe, from Sephardic Jews to Soviet dissidents.

title: A Manuscript of Ashes
author: Antonio Muñoz Molina
description: A young student in late-Franco Spain stumbles into the unsolved story of a doomed poet and the woman who loved him.

title: In Her Absence
author: Antonio Muñoz Molina
description: A provincial bureaucrat realizes that the beautiful woman he married has vanished — though she is still sitting across the table.

title: School of the Sun
author: Ana María Matute
description: A city girl is sent to live with her wealthy grandmother on Mallorca as the Spanish Civil War explodes on the mainland.

title: The Time of the Doves
author: Mercè Rodoreda
description: A Barcelona woman's quiet marriage is consumed by the Spanish Civil War in one of the great twentieth-century novels.

title: Camellia Street
author: Mercè Rodoreda
description: A foundling girl in Barcelona moves from one man's care to the next across the postwar decades.

title: Death in Spring
author: Mercè Rodoreda
description: In a nameless Catalan village where horses are suffocated in the river and men are buried in trees, a boy grows to manhood.

title: On the Edge
author: Rafael Chirbes
description: An unemployed seventy-year-old carpenter finds a body in a Valencian marshland and revisits the rotted-out promises of the Spanish property boom.

title: The Frozen Heart
author: Almudena Grandes
description: A Madrid architect at his father's funeral meets a stranger who claims to have been wronged by the family during the civil war.

title: The Ages of Lulu
author: Almudena Grandes
description: A woman's sexual awakening and descent through the bohemian demimonde of late-Franco and transition Madrid.

title: The Carpenter's Pencil
author: Manuel Rivas
description: A Republican prisoner and a Falangist doctor in civil-war Galicia share the memory of a woman and a pencil.

title: Books Burn Badly
author: Manuel Rivas
description: A sprawling chronicle of A Coruña from the Franco book-burnings through the twentieth century.

title: The Maias
author: Eça de Queirós
description: The great nineteenth-century Portuguese novel: three generations of the Maia family collapse into an aristocratic Lisbon tragedy.

title: Cousin Bazilio
author: Eça de Queirós
description: A bourgeois Lisbon wife slides into an affair with her worldly cousin while her blackmailing maid watches.

title: The Relic
author: Eça de Queirós
description: A hypocritical young Portuguese nephew journeys to the Holy Land to secure a relic — and his inheritance.

title: Jerusalem
author: Gonçalo M. Tavares
description: Five people converge on a Lisbon street one sleepless night in a small, chilly masterpiece.

title: Learning to Pray in the Age of Technique
author: Gonçalo M. Tavares
description: A famous surgeon's inability to feel anything except control becomes the novel's slow moral architecture.

title: Joseph Walser's Machine
author: Gonçalo M. Tavares
description: An ordinary factory worker maintains his collection of precise metal parts as an unnamed war moves into the city.

title: The Book of Chameleons
author: José Eduardo Agualusa
description: A Luanda man who sells invented pasts to his clients is observed by a gecko who was once a human philosopher.

title: A General Theory of Oblivion
author: José Eduardo Agualusa
description: A terrified Portuguese woman bricks herself into her Luanda apartment on the eve of Angolan independence and waits twenty-eight years.

title: Creole
author: José Eduardo Agualusa
description: A nineteenth-century Portuguese journalist in Angola chases a trader across three continents and writes him letters.

title: Nowhere to Be Found
author: Bae Suah
description: A young South Korean woman drifts through dead-end work and men as she searches for nothing in particular.

title: Untold Night and Day
author: Bae Suah
description: A Seoul audio-theater assistant wanders the summer city in a dreamlike loop where time and identity come loose.

title: A Greater Music
author: Bae Suah
description: A Korean woman in Berlin reflects on her relationship with a music teacher who has left her.

title: One Hundred Shadows
author: Hwang Jung-eun
description: Two workers in a crumbling Seoul electronics market fall in love as their shadows begin to rise up around them.

title: I'll Go On
author: Hwang Jung-eun
description: Two sisters and a lifelong friend share their fractured histories in alternating first-person voices.

title: Pavane for a Dead Princess
author: Park Min-gyu
description: A young Korean man in the 1980s falls in love with a department store colleague whose face the world finds hard to look at.

title: Castella
author: Park Min-gyu
description: Ten darkly comic stories of Seoul precarity, genre parody, and bureaucratic absurdity.

title: The Poet
author: Yi Mun-yol
description: A fictionalized life of Kim Sakkat, the nineteenth-century Korean poet who wandered his country under a bamboo hat.

title: Our Twisted Hero
author: Yi Mun-yol
description: A fifth-grade class in 1960s Korea is dominated by a monitor whose rule is a parable for authoritarian power.

title: The Wine of Astonishment
author: Earl Lovelace
description: A Trinidadian Spiritual Baptist congregation fights a colonial law that has outlawed their church.

title: Salt
author: Earl Lovelace
description: Trinidadian village life from slavery through independence, told through the griot-like voice of a schoolteacher. Commonwealth Writers' Prize winner.

title: The Dragon Can't Dance
author: Earl Lovelace
description: Carnival, poverty, and the last gasp of a hillside Port of Spain neighborhood as a steel band prepares for J'ouvert morning.

title: Segu
author: Maryse Condé
description: The 1797 collapse of a West African Bambara kingdom and the scattering of a noble family across three continents.

title: I, Tituba, Black Witch of Salem
author: Maryse Condé
description: The enslaved woman whose testimony touched off the Salem witch trials tells her own story.

title: Crossing the Mangrove
author: Maryse Condé
description: A wake for a mysterious drowned stranger in a Guadeloupean village, told in twenty voices.

title: Texaco
author: Patrick Chamoiseau
description: Three generations of Martinican history told from the squatter's shantytown of Texaco, as a city planner interviews its matriarch. Prix Goncourt winner.

title: Solibo Magnificent
author: Patrick Chamoiseau
description: A famous Martinican storyteller drops dead mid-performance and the French police try to investigate it as a murder.

title: School Days
author: Patrick Chamoiseau
description: A Creole boy's first encounter with the French colonial school system and its assault on his tongue.

title: The History of Love
author: Nicole Krauss
description: An old Polish Jewish widower in Brooklyn and a lonely New York teenager are connected by a lost novel written decades ago.

title: Great House
author: Nicole Krauss
description: Four lives revolve around a looming, many-drawered writing desk that once belonged to a vanished Chilean poet.

title: Forest Dark
author: Nicole Krauss
description: A retired New York lawyer and a novelist with writer's block both disappear into the Tel Aviv desert for reasons they cannot explain.

title: The World to Come
author: Dara Horn
description: A stolen Chagall painting links a present-day Jewish family to a Russian shtetl and the afterlife.

title: All Other Nights
author: Dara Horn
description: A young Jewish Union soldier is sent south to spy on his own Confederate relatives.

title: A Guide for the Perplexed
author: Dara Horn
description: A software developer is kidnapped in Egypt and a Cairo-Geneva manuscript cache from centuries past bears on her fate.

title: Gate of the Sun
author: Elias Khoury
description: A dying Palestinian fighter is told the story of his own people's exile by a younger man keeping vigil at his bedside.

title: As Though She Were Sleeping
author: Elias Khoury
description: A Palestinian woman living in 1940s Beirut remembers her life in three nights of dreamed sleep.

title: Gold Dust
author: Ibrahim al-Koni
description: A Tuareg man and his piebald camel flee across the Sahara with a tribal feud chasing them.

title: The Bleeding of the Stone
author: Ibrahim al-Koni
description: An Ibex-worshipping hermit in the Libyan desert defends the last waddan from hunters who will kill anything for meat.

title: Men in the Sun
author: Ghassan Kanafani
description: Three Palestinian men pay a smuggler to hide them in an empty water tanker crossing the Iraqi-Kuwaiti desert — the canonical short novel of Palestinian exile.

title: The Secret Life of Saeed: The Pessoptimist
author: Emile Habibi
description: A comical Palestinian citizen of Israel writes letters to an outer-space visitor about his absurd career as an informer — the Palestinian Candide.

title: Voices of the Lost
author: Hoda Barakat
description: Six unsent letters by Arab exiles across Europe, each found by the next writer in the chain. International Prize for Arabic Fiction winner.

title: The Sacred Night
author: Tahar Ben Jelloun
description: A Moroccan woman raised as a boy is set free by her dying father and begins a long reckoning with her body and country. Prix Goncourt winner.

title: The Sand Child
author: Tahar Ben Jelloun
description: A Moroccan father, desperate for a son, raises his eighth daughter as a boy — in the novel that precedes The Sacred Night.

title: The Yacoubian Building
author: Alaa Al Aswany
description: The inhabitants of a run-down Cairo apartment block — from dispossessed aristocrats to Islamist students — trace the state of modern Egypt.

title: Granada
author: Radwa Ashour
description: The lives of a Muslim family in Granada from the city's fall in 1492 through the early generations of forced conversion.

title: Zaat
author: Sonallah Ibrahim
description: Half the chapters narrate Zaat's ordinary Cairo life, the other half are collages of real newspaper clippings on Egyptian corruption.

title: The Committee
author: Sonallah Ibrahim
description: An Egyptian intellectual is interrogated by a shadowy committee about his career, his country, and an object he cannot name.

title: Prince Ehtejab
author: Houshang Golshiri
description: The last member of a declining Qajar noble family sits alone in his mansion recounting the cruelties of his forebears.

title: Missing Soluch
author: Mahmoud Dowlatabadi
description: In a drought-stricken Iranian village, a woman wakes to find her husband has disappeared — and she must somehow feed her children.

title: The Colonel
author: Mahmoud Dowlatabadi
description: A retired Iranian army colonel confronts the bodies of his children on a rainy night as the revolution consumes his country.

title: Censoring an Iranian Love Story
author: Shahriar Mandanipour
description: An Iranian novelist tries to write a love story that can clear the Islamic Republic's censors — and documents his own cuts as he goes.

title: Savushun
author: Simin Daneshvar
description: A landowning family in WWII Allied-occupied Shiraz contends with political chaos — the first major Persian novel by a woman.

title: My Uncle Napoleon
author: Iraj Pezeshkzad
description: An Iranian boy's conspiracy-obsessed uncle, convinced that the British are out to get him, consumes the life of the extended family — the canonical Iranian comic novel.

title: Beauty Is a Wound
author: Eka Kurniawan
description: A beautiful Indonesian prostitute rises from her grave twenty-one years after her death in a sprawling magical-realist chronicle of her country.

title: Man Tiger
author: Eka Kurniawan
description: A young Indonesian man who harbors a white tigress inside his body commits a murder — and the novel works backwards to explain why.

title: Paradise of the Blind
author: Duong Thu Huong
description: A Vietnamese woman working in a Soviet-bloc factory remembers her mother's sacrifices and the party official uncle who destroyed the family.

title: Memories of a Pure Spring
author: Duong Thu Huong
description: A Vietnamese songwriter from the resistance years collides with the disillusionments of peacetime.

title: Twilight in Jakarta
author: Mochtar Lubis
description: A panorama of corrupt 1950s Jakarta through the lives of politicians, journalists, and the urban poor who serve them.

title: Saman
author: Ayu Utami
description: The first major post-Suharto Indonesian novel — a defrocked priest, four friends, and a plantation struggle fuse into a reordering of what Indonesian fiction can do.

title: On the Road to Babadag
author: Andrzej Stasiuk
description: Stasiuk's wandering travel essays across forgotten corners of Eastern Europe — Slovakia, Albania, Moldova, Ukraine, Romania.

title: Nine
author: Andrzej Stasiuk
description: A post-communist Warsaw drifter tries to collect a debt that could save his life across a sleepless 24 hours in the city.

title: Ashes and Diamonds
author: Jerzy Andrzejewski
description: In the last days of WWII in a small Polish town, a young underground fighter is ordered to kill a communist official.

title: A Minor Apocalypse
author: Tadeusz Konwicki
description: A disillusioned Polish writer is told by friends he must burn himself to death in front of the party headquarters — and spends the day trying to find matches.

title: The Polish Complex
author: Tadeusz Konwicki
description: Polish citizens queue outside a Warsaw jewelry store on Christmas Eve while the narrator drifts through his country's historical wounds.

title: This Way for the Gas, Ladies and Gentlemen
author: Tadeusz Borowski
description: Stories drawn from Borowski's own Auschwitz imprisonment — narrated from inside the camp's grim economy of survival.

title: The Mighty Angel
author: Jerzy Pilch
description: A Polish writer's eighteenth stint in alcohol detox, told in the funniest, bleakest voice in contemporary Polish fiction.

title: His Current Woman
author: Jerzy Pilch
description: A Polish Lutheran household in a Silesian mountain town is thrown into chaos by the arrival of a silent young woman.

title: Who Was David Weiser?
author: Paweł Huelle
description: Three men who were Gdańsk schoolboys in 1957 try to reconstruct the day their classmate David Weiser vanished into the woods.

title: Mercedes-Benz
author: Paweł Huelle
description: A Polish writer's driving lessons become a meditation on his grandfather and the cars that haunt his family's twentieth century.

title: The Door
author: Magda Szabó
description: A Hungarian writer's relationship with her fierce, secretive housekeeper becomes the central moral reckoning of her life.

title: Iza's Ballad
author: Magda Szabó
description: A recently widowed Hungarian woman is brought to live with her successful doctor daughter in Budapest, and the kindness destroys her.

title: Katalin Street
author: Magda Szabó
description: Three Budapest families on one street before, during, and after the Second World War.

title: Celestial Harmonies
author: Péter Esterházy
description: A sprawling fictional history of the Esterházy dynasty braided with the author's own father — a masterpiece of Hungarian postmodernism.

title: Helping Verbs of the Heart
author: Péter Esterházy
description: A son's grief for his dead mother is rendered in a torrent of appropriated literary and philosophical quotations.

title: Sunflower
author: Gyula Krúdy
description: A young Budapest woman withdraws to her late grandmother's countryside estate and is courted by a parade of grotesques.

title: The Adventures of Sindbad
author: Gyula Krúdy
description: A long-dead Hungarian lover named Sindbad returns from the grave across twenty sketches to revisit the women he once haunted.

title: They Were Counted
author: Miklós Bánffy
description: The first volume of the Transylvanian Trilogy: two Hungarian cousins and the doomed decade before WWI in Austro-Hungarian Transylvania.

title: They Were Found Wanting
author: Miklós Bánffy
description: The second volume follows the cousins deeper into Budapest politics and their separate tragedies.

title: They Were Divided
author: Miklós Bánffy
description: The Transylvanian Trilogy closes with the outbreak of the First World War and the scattering of the remnants.

title: Metropole
author: Ferenc Karinthy
description: A Hungarian linguist arriving at a conference lands in a country whose language he cannot decode, and cannot leave.

title: The Sinistra Zone
author: Ádám Bodor
description: In a cold militarized zone in the Carpathians, a stranger tries to find his adopted son under a regime that has erased him.

title: The Slynx
author: Tatyana Tolstaya
description: Two centuries after the Blast, Moscow has reverted to a wooden-village barbarism where Pushkin is worshipped and a monstrous Slynx prowls the forest.

title: Omon Ra
author: Viktor Pelevin
description: A Soviet boy who dreams of space joins a cosmonaut program that turns out to be a suicidal Potemkin propaganda operation.

title: Homo Zapiens
author: Viktor Pelevin
description: A post-Soviet advertising copywriter discovers that Russia itself is being rendered inside a simulated reality run by magic.

title: The Life of Insects
author: Viktor Pelevin
description: In a seaside Crimean resort, humans and insects merge and switch places in a series of fables about post-Soviet life.

title: Moscow to the End of the Line
author: Venedikt Yerofeev
description: A drunk narrator tries to ride a commuter train from Moscow to the suburb of Petushki — the cult masterpiece of late-Soviet samizdat.

title: The Big Green Tent
author: Lyudmila Ulitskaya
description: Three Moscow boys grow up in the thaw generation and lose their lives to the dissident movement.

title: Daniel Stein, Interpreter
author: Lyudmila Ulitskaya
description: A Polish Jew who survived the war as a translator for the Nazis becomes an Israeli Catholic priest — a novel told in documents.

title: There Once Lived a Woman Who Tried to Kill Her Neighbor's Baby
author: Lyudmila Petrushevskaya
description: Scary fairy tales set in the communal apartments of the late Soviet Union.

title: There Once Lived a Girl Who Seduced Her Sister's Husband, and He Hanged Himself
author: Lyudmila Petrushevskaya
description: Love stories from the same bitter, compassionate observer of Russian domestic life.

title: Laurus
author: Yevgeny Vodolazkin
description: A fifteenth-century Russian healer spends his life atoning for the death of his beloved — in a novel that braids medieval Russian with the contemporary.

title: Maidenhair
author: Mikhail Shishkin
description: A Russian interpreter in Swiss asylum hearings listens to Chechen refugees as his own letters home unspool.

title: The Light and the Dark
author: Mikhail Shishkin
description: A Russian conscript in the 1900 Boxer expedition and a contemporary Russian woman write love letters across time.

title: Death and the Penguin
author: Andrey Kurkov
description: A struggling Kyiv obituary writer, who keeps a pet penguin acquired from a failing zoo, begins writing obituaries for people who are still alive.

title: Bosnian Chronicle
author: Ivo Andrić
description: The French, Austrian, and Ottoman consuls navigate a tense decade in nineteenth-century Ottoman Travnik — by the 1961 Nobel laureate.

title: On the Edge of Reason
author: Miroslav Krleža
description: An insulted Zagreb lawyer tells the truth about his dinner companions for one night, and his life collapses.

title: The Return of Philip Latinowicz
author: Miroslav Krleža
description: A Zagreb painter returns home after decades abroad to confront the provincial society that shaped him.

title: The Museum of Unconditional Surrender
author: Dubravka Ugrešić
description: A Yugoslav writer exiled to Berlin collects fragments, photographs, and her mother's story as a Yugoslavia ceases to exist.

title: Baba Yaga Laid an Egg
author: Dubravka Ugrešić
description: A triptych of tales about old women and Slavic folklore, culminating in an essay-decoding of the whole book.

title: S.: A Novel about the Balkans
author: Slavenka Drakulić
description: A Bosnian Muslim woman in a Swedish hospital remembers the Serbian rape camps where she survived.

title: They Would Never Hurt a Fly
author: Slavenka Drakulić
description: Drakulić sits in the Hague war crimes tribunal and studies the astonishing ordinariness of the accused.

title: Death and the Dervish
author: Meša Selimović
description: An Ottoman-era Bosnian Sufi dervish's quiet life is shattered when his brother is arrested — the canonical Bosnian novel.

title: The Lazarus Project
author: Aleksandar Hemon
description: A Sarajevan émigré in Chicago investigates the 1908 shooting of a young Jewish immigrant by the city's police chief.

title: Nowhere Man
author: Aleksandar Hemon
description: A Bosnian wanderer named Jozef Pronek passes through the lives of everyone he meets in Kiev, Chicago, and Sarajevo.

title: Bait
author: David Albahari
description: A Serbian émigré in Canada listens to tape recordings his mother made before her death and tries to write her life.

title: The Hooligan's Return
author: Norman Manea
description: A Romanian Jewish writer returns to Bucharest after decades of exile and contends with everything his country has tried to forget.

title: Captives
author: Norman Manea
description: A three-part exploration of life under Ceaușescu's Romania, Manea's first major novel.

title: Love in a Fallen City
author: Eileen Chang
description: A divorced Shanghainese woman meets a wealthy playboy on the eve of the Japanese occupation of Hong Kong — the title novella collected with five more. NYRB Classics.

title: Lust, Caution
author: Eileen Chang
description: A young woman in wartime Shanghai is recruited to seduce and assassinate a collaborator.

title: The Rouge of the North
author: Eileen Chang
description: A Chinese woman sold into a wealthy family slowly becomes the tyrant she once feared.

title: Rickshaw Boy
author: Lao She
description: A young rickshaw puller in 1920s Beijing saves to buy his own vehicle and is undone by war and circumstance — the canonical Chinese realist novel.

title: Mr. Ma and Son
author: Lao She
description: A widowed Chinese father and his son inherit an antique shop in 1920s London, where they encounter English racism and their own homesickness.

title: Border Town
author: Shen Congwen
description: A ferryman's granddaughter in a remote West Hunan town is courted by two brothers — the canonical work of Chinese rural modernism.

title: The Four Books
author: Yan Lianke
description: A re-education camp during the Great Leap Forward, told through four interlocking manuscripts that were banned in China.

title: Serve the People!
author: Yan Lianke
description: A Maoist division commander's young wife begins an affair with her husband's cook amid slogans of revolutionary discipline.

title: Dream of Ding Village
author: Yan Lianke
description: A Chinese village poisoned by an AIDS-infected blood-selling scheme is narrated by a dead child.

title: Raise the Red Lantern
author: Su Tong
description: A young university woman becomes the fourth concubine of a wealthy man in 1920s China and enters a household war.

title: Rice
author: Su Tong
description: A refugee from a famine-stricken village rises through the rice trade of a decadent pre-Communist city.

title: My Life as Emperor
author: Su Tong
description: A fictional boy emperor of an invented Chinese dynasty slides from puppet ruler to tightrope walker.

title: The Song of Everlasting Sorrow
author: Wang Anyi
description: A 1940s Shanghai beauty queen's life across four decades of Chinese history — the canonical novel of modern Shanghai.

title: Family
author: Ba Jin
description: Three brothers in a 1920s Sichuan Confucian household rebel against their patriarch's rule — the canonical Chinese anti-traditional novel.

title: The Invisibility Cloak
author: Ge Fei
description: A Beijing hi-fi amplifier maker's quiet life is upended when a wealthy client asks him to build a machine for a hidden villa.

title: The Hunting Gun
author: Yasushi Inoue
description: A writer receives three letters that tell the same doomed love affair from three women's perspectives.

title: Bullfight
author: Yasushi Inoue
description: An Osaka newspaper sponsors a massive postwar bullfighting tournament, and everything goes wrong.

title: The Blue Wolf
author: Yasushi Inoue
description: A novelistic life of Genghis Khan as a man who never knew whether he was the son of his father.

title: The Waiting Years
author: Fumiko Enchi
description: A Meiji-era Japanese wife is sent to recruit concubines for her tyrannical husband and endures decades of quiet rage.

title: Masks
author: Fumiko Enchi
description: A young Japanese widow is slowly shaped by her formidable mother-in-law into an instrument of revenge.

title: A True Novel
author: Minae Mizumura
description: A Japanese Wuthering Heights: a postwar Japanese boy's consuming love for the daughter of a wealthy family.

title: An I-Novel
author: Minae Mizumura
description: A bilingual Japanese woman in an Ivy League graduate program spends a day on the phone with her sister, remembering their American exile.

title: Inheritance from Mother
author: Minae Mizumura
description: An aging Japanese woman reckons with her difficult, recently dead mother while on a hot-springs retreat near Mount Fuji.

title: Fires on the Plain
author: Shohei Ooka
description: A tubercular Japanese soldier at the end of WWII wanders the Philippine jungle toward starvation, cannibalism, and grace.

title: Taken Captive
author: Shohei Ooka
description: Ooka's memoir of his capture and internment by American forces in the Philippines — the raw material of Fires on the Plain.

title: Into a Black Sun
author: Takeshi Kaiko
description: A Japanese war correspondent in Vietnam spirals into alcohol and fatalism as the war grinds on.

title: Darkness in Summer
author: Takeshi Kaiko
description: A Japanese writer exiled in Europe is visited by his former lover and they spend a restless summer that can only end badly.

title: Notes of a Crocodile
author: Qiu Miaojin
description: A queer Taiwanese college student's campus journal in 1980s Taipei — a canonical Chinese-language lesbian novel.

title: Last Words from Montmartre
author: Qiu Miaojin
description: A young woman in Paris writes twenty unsent letters to her lover in the weeks before her suicide.

title: Taipei People
author: Pai Hsien-yung
description: Fourteen linked stories about mainland Chinese exiles living in Taipei — the canonical work of Taiwanese fiction.

title: Crystal Boys
author: Pai Hsien-yung
description: Young gay men in 1970s Taipei gather in a park that becomes their only possible home.

title: A Mind at Peace
author: Ahmet Hamdi Tanpınar
description: A young Turkish intellectual's consciousness drifts across Istanbul and old Ottoman music on the eve of WWII — the canonical Turkish modernist novel.

title: The Time Regulation Institute
author: Ahmet Hamdi Tanpınar
description: A Turkish bureaucratic agency is established to synchronize all the clocks of the country in Tanpınar's comic masterwork.

title: Madonna in a Fur Coat
author: Sabahattin Ali
description: A young Turkish man in 1920s Berlin falls for a Jewish artist whose portrait of a Madonna resembles herself — a rediscovered Turkish classic.

title: Berji Kristin: Tales from the Garbage Hills
author: Latife Tekin
description: Squatters on the outskirts of Istanbul build a gecekondu neighborhood from the city's dumps.

title: The Last Jew
author: Yoram Kaniuk
description: A Holocaust survivor and his son try to hold onto Jewish memory in Israel and beyond.

title: Adam Resurrected
author: Yoram Kaniuk
description: A former circus clown who survived a Nazi camp by acting as the commandant's dog ends up in an Israeli asylum for Holocaust survivors.

title: The Blue Mountain
author: Meir Shalev
description: A grandson of Russian Jewish pioneers recounts the mythic early years of the Jezreel Valley moshav his grandfather helped found.

title: A Pigeon and a Boy
author: Meir Shalev
description: A carrier-pigeon message from a dying Israeli soldier in 1948 threads through two generations of unresolved love.

title: Dolly City
author: Orly Castel-Bloom
description: A possibly mad doctor in a future Tel Aviv adopts a baby she finds in a plastic bag and subjects him to increasingly bizarre care.

title: Human Parts
author: Orly Castel-Bloom
description: Early-2000s Israel during the Second Intifada, seen through the lives of a half-dozen ordinary Tel Avivians.

title: Dancing Arabs
author: Sayed Kashua
description: A Palestinian Israeli boy from a Triangle village attends a Jewish boarding school and spends his life trying to vanish between identities.

title: Let It Be Morning
author: Sayed Kashua
description: A Palestinian Israeli journalist returns to his home village just as the Israeli army seals it off from the outside world.

title: The Shipyard
author: Juan Carlos Onetti
description: A middle-aged drifter returns to a rotting Uruguayan port and takes over the management of a long-defunct shipyard.

title: A Brief Life
author: Juan Carlos Onetti
description: A failing Buenos Aires advertising man invents a second life in an imaginary town called Santa María — the novel that established Onetti's imaginary world.

title: Artificial Respiration
author: Ricardo Piglia
description: A young Argentine writer corresponds with his exiled uncle in a novel about history, dictatorship, and Borges.

title: Target in the Night
author: Ricardo Piglia
description: A Puerto Rican businessman is murdered in a small Argentine pampas town, and the investigation opens the town's political fault lines.

title: The Sixty-Five Years of Washington
author: Juan José Saer
description: Two Argentine friends walk across a provincial city discussing a birthday party neither of them attended.

title: Scars
author: Juan José Saer
description: Four narrators each tell one part of the same Argentine small-town murder.

title: My Two Worlds
author: Sergio Chejfec
description: An Argentine writer in a Brazilian city wanders through a park on the eve of his birthday in one extended reverie.

title: The Museum of Eterna's Novel
author: Macedonio Fernández
description: An Argentine proto-Borgesian puzzle: 57 prefaces followed by a novel that is supposed to be, and pointedly isn't, written.

title: Piano Stories
author: Felisberto Hernández
description: Uncanny tales from the Uruguayan writer Calvino called one of his masters — pianists, puppets, haunted houses.

title: I the Supreme
author: Augusto Roa Bastos
description: A dictator-narrator dictates his self-justification as his country crumbles around him — Paraguay's canonical dictator novel.

title: Son of Man
author: Augusto Roa Bastos
description: Linked tales of Paraguayan peasant suffering from the nineteenth century through the Chaco War.

title: Three Trapped Tigers
author: Guillermo Cabrera Infante
description: The 1958 Havana nightlife of three young Cubans, told in a torrent of bilingual puns and Caribbean slang.

title: Infante's Inferno
author: Guillermo Cabrera Infante
description: A Havana boy's chronology of his own sexual misadventures from early childhood through his escape into exile.

title: Havana Blue
author: Leonardo Padura
description: Havana police detective Mario Conde investigates the disappearance of an old classmate turned corrupt bureaucrat — the first Havana Quartet novel.

title: The Man Who Loved Dogs
author: Leonardo Padura
description: A Cuban writer investigates the Spanish exile who murdered Trotsky in Mexico — Padura's masterpiece.

title: Heretics
author: Leonardo Padura
description: A Cuban Jew's journey through the Inquisition, the St. Louis refugee ship, and twenty-first century Havana.

title: God's Bits of Wood
author: Sembène Ousmane
description: The 1947 railway workers' strike from Dakar to Bamako, told through the lives of the African families sustaining it.

title: Black Docker
author: Sembène Ousmane
description: A Senegalese dockworker in postwar Marseille finishes a novel about his grandmother and is accused of plagiarism.

title: Xala
author: Sembène Ousmane
description: A Senegalese businessman's third marriage is spoiled by impotence — a sharp satire of postcolonial African elites.

title: Ambiguous Adventure
author: Cheikh Hamidou Kane
description: A young Senegalese Muslim is sent by his family to France for a Western education and loses himself between two worlds.

title: The Poor Christ of Bomba
author: Mongo Beti
description: A Cameroonian altar boy records his missionary priest's tour of a rural diocese that does not want him.

title: Mission to Kala
author: Mongo Beti
description: A failed French lycée student is sent to his ancestral village to retrieve a runaway cousin's wife and becomes a hero.

title: King Lazarus
author: Mongo Beti
description: A dying Cameroonian chief converts to Catholicism and tries to dismiss his many wives, upending his kingdom.

title: Houseboy
author: Ferdinand Oyono
description: A young Cameroonian servant's diary records the final days of French colonial rule from inside a commandant's house.

title: The Old Man and the Medal
author: Ferdinand Oyono
description: An illiterate Cameroonian elder is to be honored by the French colonial government and learns what the honor is worth.

title: Life and a Half
author: Sony Labou Tansi
description: A Central African dictator is haunted by his slaughtered political rival through generations of subsequent tyrants.

title: The Seven Solitudes of Lorsa Lopez
author: Sony Labou Tansi
description: A murder in a small African town stops time as the townspeople wait decades for the authorities to arrive.

title: Butterfly Burning
author: Yvonne Vera
description: A young woman in 1940s Bulawayo is caught in an affair with an older man as colonial Rhodesia forbids her a future.

title: The Stone Virgins
author: Yvonne Vera
description: Two sisters in a Matabeleland village survive the Zimbabwean civil war and a dissident's attack.

title: Without a Name
author: Yvonne Vera
description: A Zimbabwean woman flees a rural village for Harare with her baby and is undone by what she has done to survive.

title: Johnny Mad Dog
author: Emmanuel Dongala
description: A teenage African militiaman and a schoolgirl on the run tell alternating chapters of a civil war.

title: The Fire of Origins
author: Emmanuel Dongala
description: A Congolese shaman and his descendants move from precolonial life through French colonization into independence.

title: Jakob von Gunten
author: Robert Walser
description: A young aristocrat enrolls in a school that trains servants and observes its mysterious headmaster — Kafka considered Walser a master.

title: The Tanners
author: Robert Walser
description: The Tanner siblings drift through turn-of-the-century Switzerland in Walser's first novel — NYRB Classics.

title: Memoirs of an Anti-Semite
author: Gregor von Rezzori
description: Five connected stories of a Romanian-Austrian aristocrat's life of casual prejudice across the twentieth century.

title: The Snows of Yesteryear
author: Gregor von Rezzori
description: A memoir of Rezzori's Czernowitz childhood, rendered as five portraits of his family and its servants.

title: The Strudlhof Steps
author: Heimito von Doderer
description: A vast Viennese novel centered on a famous staircase, weaving scores of characters across two decades — NYRB Classics 2021.

title: Storm of Steel
author: Ernst Jünger

title: On the Marble Cliffs
author: Ernst Jünger
description: An allegorical novel of a coming totalitarian invasion, written in plain sight under the Nazi regime.

title: Dream Story
author: Arthur Schnitzler
description: A Viennese doctor wanders into a secret masked orgy after his wife confesses a fantasy — the basis for Eyes Wide Shut.

title: Late Fame
author: Arthur Schnitzler
description: A long-forgotten Viennese poet is suddenly adored by a younger literary circle and comes to understand what they really want from him.

title: Tales of a Long Night
author: Alfred Döblin
description: Döblin's last novel: a British veteran returns from WWII, and his family's response unfolds in nested stories over a single night.

title: Malina

description: A woman in Vienna splits between two men — her violent father and the cold rational Malina — in Bachmann's canonical novel.

title: Auto-da-Fé
author: Elias Canetti
description: A reclusive Viennese sinologist is manipulated into marrying his housekeeper and slowly driven from his own library — by the 1981 Nobel laureate.

title: Every Man Dies Alone
author: Hans Fallada
description: A working-class Berlin couple mount a quiet private resistance to the Nazi regime by scattering handwritten postcards across the city.

title: The House by the Medlar Tree
author: Giovanni Verga
description: A Sicilian fishing family's slow ruin across several generations — the canonical work of Italian verismo.

title: Little Novels of Sicily
author: Giovanni Verga
description: Short stories of Sicilian village life drawn with Verga's unsparing realist eye.

title: The Viceroys
author: Federico De Roberto
description: A corrupt Sicilian noble family adapts across the nineteenth century to retain power through every political regime.

title: Bread and Wine
author: Ignazio Silone
description: A communist organizer in fascist Italy disguises himself as a priest and hides in a rural village.

title: Fontamara
author: Ignazio Silone
description: An isolated Abruzzi village is ground under the boot of local fascist authority — the canonical Italian peasant novel of the 1930s.

title: Conversation in Sicily
author: Elio Vittorini
description: A Sicilian printer living in Milan returns home to his mother and encounters ghosts of a country crushed by fascism.

title: Women of Messina
author: Elio Vittorini
description: A group of refugees rebuilds a war-ruined Sicilian village in Vittorini's postwar romance.

title: Neapolitan Chronicles
author: Anna Maria Ortese
description: Linked stories of postwar Naples — Europa Editions — the book Elena Ferrante cites as formative.

title: The Iguana
author: Anna Maria Ortese
description: A Milanese count lands on an uncharted Portuguese island where an iguana is kept as a servant.

title: Johnny the Partisan
author: Beppe Fenoglio
description: A young Italian anti-fascist joins the partisans in the Langhe hills and endures the 1943-45 war.

title: A Private Affair
author: Beppe Fenoglio
description: A partisan near the end of the Italian civil war searches obsessively for a friend who may have slept with the girl he loved.

title: The Beautiful Antonio
author: Vitaliano Brancati
description: A famously beautiful Catania man's arranged marriage exposes a secret that shames his whole family.

title: The Late Mattia Pascal
author: Luigi Pirandello
description: A Sicilian man reads in the newspaper that he has drowned and decides to start a new life as somebody else.

title: One, No One and One Hundred Thousand
author: Luigi Pirandello
description: A Sicilian man's wife casually observes that his nose is crooked, and his identity unspools completely.

title: Words Are Stones
author: Carlo Levi
description: Levi's travels through Sicily after Christ Stopped at Eboli — the writer bears witness to three trips to the island.

title: The Following Story
author: Cees Nooteboom
description: A Dutch classical scholar falls asleep in Amsterdam and wakes up in a Lisbon hotel room — then begins a posthumous journey.

title: Rituals
author: Cees Nooteboom
description: A privileged Amsterdam bystander drifts through his life watching two very different father-son pairs destroy themselves.

title: Lost Paradise
author: Cees Nooteboom
description: Two Brazilian women travel to Australia in search of angels and end up working as actors in a Perth theater.

title: The Assault
author: Harry Mulisch
description: A Dutch boy whose family is murdered by Nazi reprisal spends his life trying to piece together what happened that night.

title: The Discovery of Heaven
author: Harry Mulisch
description: Two angels intervene in the lives of three Dutch friends to retrieve the stone tablets of the Ten Commandments from Earth.

title: Siegfried
author: Harry Mulisch
description: An aging Dutch writer meets an elderly Austrian couple who claim to have been in Hitler's inner circle.

title: The Darkroom of Damocles
author: Willem Frederik Hermans
description: A Dutch tobacconist is drawn into the wartime resistance by a mysterious double who may or may not exist.

title: Beyond Sleep
author: Willem Frederik Hermans
description: A Dutch graduate student joins a Norwegian geology expedition in the Arctic and finds only catastrophe.

title: The Evenings
author: Gerard Reve
description: The last ten evenings of 1946 in the life of a bored Amsterdam office worker — the canonical Dutch postwar novel.

title: Parents Worry
author: Gerard Reve
description: A middle-aged Dutch writer and his younger lover pass a summer in rural France.

title: The Black Lake
author: Hella Haasse
description: A Dutch colonial boy in Java and his Indonesian childhood friend grow up on opposite sides of an independence movement.

title: In a Dark Wood Wandering
author: Hella Haasse
description: The life of Charles d'Orléans, the fifteenth-century poet-duke, told in meticulous detail across European battlefields and prisons.

title: The Hidden Force
author: Louis Couperus
description: A Dutch colonial resident in Java is slowly undone by a native mystical force he refuses to believe in.

title: Eline Vere
author: Louis Couperus
description: A beautiful young woman in The Hague society slowly wastes away under the pressure of her own expectations.

title: The Sorrow of Belgium
author: Hugo Claus
description: A Flemish boy grows up during the Nazi occupation in a family that is itself complicit — the canonical modern Belgian novel.

title: Desire
author: Hugo Claus
description: Two Flemish small-town gamblers travel to Las Vegas and destroy each other.

title: Tirza
author: Arnon Grunberg
description: A recently retired Amsterdam book editor fixates on his departing daughter Tirza in a steadily darker monologue.

title: Lanark
author: Alasdair Gray
description: A dystopian Glasgow named Unthank mirrors and swallows the real city in Gray's four-part postmodernist masterpiece.

title: 1982, Janine
author: Alasdair Gray
description: A middle-aged Scottish security executive lies sleepless in a hotel and works through pornographic fantasies and political guilt.

title: Poor Things
author: Alasdair Gray
description: A Victorian Glasgow doctor reanimates a drowned woman with the brain of her unborn child. Whitbread Prize winner.

title: Day
author: A.L. Kennedy
description: A WWII RAF tail gunner takes a job as an extra in a prisoner-of-war film and relives the war.

title: Paradise
author: A.L. Kennedy
description: A functioning alcoholic Scottish woman's blackouts are slowly narrowing her life.

title: The Panopticon
author: Jenni Fagan
description: A fifteen-year-old Scottish girl is sent to an experimental institution for chronic young offenders and refuses to surrender.

title: The Sunlight Pilgrims
author: Jenni Fagan
description: In a near-future Scotland heading into an unstoppable ice age, a grieving young man moves into a caravan park.

title: The Land of Spices
author: Kate O'Brien
description: A Reverend Mother at an Irish convent boarding school watches over a young student who reminds her of her own past.

title: Mary Lavelle
author: Kate O'Brien
description: A young Irish governess working for a wealthy family in northern Spain falls in love with their married son.

title: Good Behaviour
author: Molly Keane
description: An aging Anglo-Irish spinster recalls her childhood in a decaying Big House — Booker-shortlisted comic masterpiece.

title: Time After Time
author: Molly Keane
description: Three elderly siblings and their blind sister-in-law live out the decline of their Irish country estate.

title: Proxopera
author: Benedict Kiely
description: A retired Northern Irish schoolteacher is forced by the IRA to drive a bomb into town as a proxy.

title: The Captain with the Whiskers
author: Benedict Kiely
description: A tyrannical Irish captain's shadow rules his children's lives long after his death.

title: Langrishe, Go Down
author: Aidan Higgins
description: Three spinster sisters in a decaying Irish country house look back on a love affair that briefly broke their silence.

title: Balcony of Europe
author: Aidan Higgins
description: An Irish painter living on the Costa del Sol in the 1960s records the affairs and debaucheries of an expatriate colony.

title: The Butcher Boy
author: Patrick McCabe
description: A young boy in a small Irish town in the 1960s narrates his own slide into murder in a ferocious voice.

title: Breakfast on Pluto
author: Patrick McCabe
description: A transgender Irish woman from a border town tells her own story through 1970s London.

title: Mean Spirit
author: Linda Hogan
description: Osage people in 1920s Oklahoma are murdered for their oil rights in Hogan's Pulitzer-finalist novel.

title: Solar Storms
author: Linda Hogan
description: A teenage Chickasaw girl returns to the Minnesota-Canadian border to her great-grandmothers' land as a dam project threatens their community.

title: Bearheart
author: Gerald Vizenor
description: A group of native travelers make a post-apocalyptic pilgrimage across the American heartland in Vizenor's trickster novel.

title: Griever: An American Monkey King in China
author: Gerald Vizenor
description: An Anishinaabe exchange teacher in Tianjin reinvents himself as a figure out of Chinese mythology.

title: The Royal Physician's Visit
author: Per Olov Enquist
description: A German doctor becomes the confidant of an unstable Danish king and regent for his kingdom in 1768 — the novel that made Enquist internationally famous.

title: The Book About Blanche and Marie
author: Per Olov Enquist
description: Marie Curie and her friend Blanche Wittman, once Charcot's most famous hysteric patient, share their love and research.

title: The Emigrants
author: Vilhelm Moberg
description: A nineteenth-century Swedish farming family decides to emigrate to America — the first volume of the canonical Swedish emigrant saga.

title: Unto a Good Land
author: Vilhelm Moberg
description: The Swedish emigrants cross the Atlantic and journey through America to Minnesota.

title: A Fugitive Crosses His Tracks
author: Aksel Sandemose
description: A Danish-Norwegian murderer writes his memoir from the vanishing point where his guilt becomes unreadable — the novel that coined the Law of Jante.

title: The Adversary
author: Emmanuel Carrère
description: Carrère reconstructs the true story of a Frenchman who murdered his entire family after years of pretending to be a doctor at the WHO.

title: Limonov
author: Emmanuel Carrère
description: A biography of the Russian writer Eduard Limonov — poet, derelict, dissident, nationalist politician, soldier — by the French master of literary nonfiction.

title: The Kingdom
author: Emmanuel Carrère
description: Carrère investigates early Christianity with the obsessive attention he brings to every subject, reconstructing Luke and Paul's lives.

title: Lives Other Than My Own
author: Emmanuel Carrère
description: Two deaths — a child in the 2004 tsunami and his sister-in-law — become the occasion for a meditation on how to live among other people's griefs.

title: Three Strong Women
author: Marie NDiaye
description: Three linked novellas about Senegalese-French women navigating impossible families and betrayals. Prix Goncourt winner.

title: My Heart Hemmed In
author: Marie NDiaye
description: A Bordeaux schoolteacher and her husband discover that their neighbors have inexplicably begun to hate them.

title: Ladivine
author: Marie NDiaye
description: A French woman visits her mother in secret once a month — and never tells her own family the mother exists.

title: HHhH
author: Laurent Binet
description: The 1942 assassination of Reinhard Heydrich by Czech and Slovak partisans, told through a self-aware metafictional investigation.

title: The Seventh Function of Language
author: Laurent Binet
description: Roland Barthes's 1980 death becomes the opening of a secret war among structuralist philosophers.

title: Civilizations
author: Laurent Binet
description: An alternate history in which the Incas conquer Europe instead of the other way around.

title: Vernon Subutex 1
author: Virginie Despentes
description: A former Parisian record-shop owner evicted into homelessness drifts through the couch-surfing networks of his past — portrait of a crumbling France.

title: Apocalypse Baby
author: Virginie Despentes
description: A Parisian private detective and a teenage runaway investigate a missing-persons case across an underclass landscape.

title: The Perfect Nanny
author: Leïla Slimani
description: A Parisian nanny murders the two children she cares for — Slimani's Prix Goncourt novel opens with the end.

title: In the Country of Others
author: Leïla Slimani
description: A French woman follows her Moroccan husband back to his family's farm during the French-Moroccan decolonization.

title: Plague and Cholera
author: Patrick Deville
description: A novelistic life of Alexandre Yersin, the Swiss-French bacteriologist who identified the plague bacillus.

title: Viva
author: Patrick Deville
description: Trotsky, Tina Modotti, and Frida Kahlo's Mexico City converges in Deville's signature docu-fiction.

title: Zone
author: Mathias Énard
description: A Franco-Croatian secret agent on a train from Milan to Rome reviews a lifetime of Mediterranean atrocities in one unbroken sentence.

title: Compass
author: Mathias Énard
description: A sleepless Viennese musicologist walks through the Orientalist tradition of his field and the woman he never quite loved. Prix Goncourt winner.

title: Tell Them of Battles, Kings, and Elephants
author: Mathias Énard
description: A young Michelangelo travels to Constantinople in 1506 to design a bridge for Sultan Bayezid II.

title: The Heart
author: Maylis de Kerangal
description: A single day in the life of a transplant heart — from a French surfer's accident to a new recipient.

title: Painting Time
author: Maylis de Kerangal
description: A young French woman trains as a trompe-l'œil painter in an elite Brussels atelier.

title: The Order of the Day
author: Éric Vuillard
description: The 1938 Anschluss reconstructed from the meetings at which Hitler's cabinet quietly negotiated the annexation of Austria. Prix Goncourt winner.

title: The War of the Poor
author: Éric Vuillard
description: A brief history of Thomas Müntzer's peasant uprising in Reformation Germany.

title: The End of Eddy
author: Édouard Louis
description: A young gay boy grows up in a poor, violent northern French village and eventually escapes.

title: A Man's Place
author: Édouard Louis
description: Louis reckons with his father's broken body and the French state that broke it.

title: The Egoist
author: George Meredith
description: A wealthy Victorian man's casual selfishness is slowly exposed by his fiancée, in Meredith's masterpiece of domestic comedy.

title: The Ordeal of Richard Feverel
author: George Meredith
description: A young Victorian heir is raised under his father's elaborate pedagogical System, which destroys him when he finally falls in love.

title: New Grub Street
author: George Gissing
description: Two Victorian writers — one an artist, one a hack — grind through literary London's impossible economy.

title: The Odd Women
author: George Gissing
description: The sisters and single women of late-Victorian London navigate a society that has no place for them.

title: Erewhon
author: Samuel Butler
description: A Victorian traveler discovers a hidden country where illness is a crime and crime is an illness.

title: The Way of All Flesh
author: Samuel Butler
description: Butler's posthumous novel dissects four generations of a hypocritical English clerical family.

title: The Enchanted Wanderer
author: Nikolai Leskov
description: A Russian monk tells a lifetime of adventures — serfdom, horse trading, captivity among Tatars, and a failed vocation.

title: Lady Macbeth of Mtsensk
author: Nikolai Leskov
description: A provincial Russian merchant's wife slips into murder and ruin to protect her illicit affair with a servant.

title: My Past and Thoughts
author: Alexander Herzen
description: The canonical memoir of the nineteenth-century Russian revolutionary in exile — Isaiah Berlin considered it essential reading.

title: Carmen and Other Stories
author: Prosper Mérimée
description: The collected stories, including Carmen (the source of Bizet's opera) and Colomba.

title: Letters from My Windmill
author: Alphonse Daudet
description: Sketches of Provençal life from a Parisian writer who bought an old windmill as a retreat.

title: Tartarin of Tarascon
author: Alphonse Daudet
description: A provincial French braggart travels to North Africa to hunt lions — Daudet's comic satire of Second Empire fantasy.

title: The Method
author: Juli Zeh
description: In a health-optimized near-future Germany, a biologist is prosecuted for the sin of refusing to monitor her own body.

title: Unter Leuten
author: Juli Zeh
description: A small Brandenburg village is thrown into chaos by a planned wind-turbine project and the people who move to town.

title: Before the Feast
author: Saša Stanišić
